package repository

import (
	"log"
	"net/http"

	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Anti-pattern: Repository logs with HTTP status codes and route labels.
// Repositories should log domain-level events, not transport metadata.

type Session struct {
	ID     string `gorm:"column:id"`
	UserID string `gorm:"column:user_id"`
	Token  string `gorm:"column:token"`
}

type SessionRepository struct {
	db *gorm.DB
}

// Create logs HTTP status codes - anti-pattern.
func (r *SessionRepository) Create(session *Session) error {
	err := r.db.Create(session).Error
	if err != nil {
		log.Printf("[ERROR] route=/api/v1/sessions status=%d error=%v", http.StatusInternalServerError, err)
		return err
	}
	log.Printf("[INFO] route=/api/v1/sessions status=%d session_id=%s", http.StatusCreated, session.ID)
	return nil
}

// FindByToken logs route names and HTTP status - anti-pattern.
func (r *SessionRepository) FindByToken(token string) (*Session, error) {
	var session Session
	err := r.db.Where("token = ?", token).First(&session).Error
	if err != nil {
		log.Printf("[ERROR] route=/api/v1/sessions/lookup status=%d token=%s", http.StatusNotFound, token)
		return nil, err
	}
	log.Printf("[INFO] route=/api/v1/sessions/lookup status=%d", http.StatusOK)
	return &session, nil
}

// Delete logs with HTTP route and status labels - anti-pattern.
func (r *SessionRepository) Delete(id string) error {
	result := r.db.Delete(&Session{}, "id = ?", id)
	log.Printf("[INFO] route=DELETE /api/v1/sessions/:id status=%d rows=%d", http.StatusOK, result.RowsAffected)
	return result.Error
}
